extra.reasoning: The property requires that rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are never both true. rec_drug_a is set true only by rule rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), which requires prior_gi_bleed AND asthma AND ckd_dialysis AND qt_prolongation AND low_ejection_fraction AND bradycardia all true. rec_drug_b is set true only by rule rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50), which requires prior_gi_bleed. Crucially, prior_gi_bleed is one of the conjuncts required for rec_drug_a, so any input that fires rec_a_from_conjunction necessarily also fires rec_b_from_trigger. Each output has exactly one rule that can set it true, so there is no conflicting rule or priority/order tiebreak that could reset either to false, and the defaults apply only to unset outputs. Setting all six conjuncts true (diabetes is irrelevant; chosen false, affects only rec_drug_c) yields rec_drug_a = true and rec_drug_b = true simultaneously, violating mutual exclusion.
